title: John Swinney accused of 'sitting on his hands' while US uses Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Ross Greer accused John Swinney of inaction (Image: Holyrood TV) JOHN Swinney has been accused of "sitting on his hands" and urged to finally ban US troops from Scottish Government -owned airports amid the US-Israeli war on Iran. Speaking at First Minister’s Questions, Scottish Greens co-leader Ross Greer claimed that flight data compiled by the party shows that 24 US military flights landed at Prestwick Airport in the week leading up to Trump’s bombing of Iran , including at least one that came from Israel.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: “Trump and Netanyahu are not liberating the Iranian people from their brutal regime by murdering their children – as they did when they killed dozens of little girls in a Tehran school shortly after they arrived for class on Saturday,” he said. READ MORE: Major new Holyrood poll predicts huge SNP win as party just short of majority “The Israeli military is already rightly banned from using Prestwick Airport because of their genocidal assault on Palestine . The First Minister has said that America and Israel’s attack on Iran is not compatible with the international rules-based system. In other words, it is illegal under international law.”

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Greer then asked: “So surely he will now ban the American military from Prestwick Airport?” Swinney responded by reiterating that he “cannot see how the intervention by the United States and Israel is compatible with an international rules-based system”.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney accused of 'sitting on his hands' while US uses Prestwick Airport
